title: Muhlenbergia leptoura
taxonomy: Muhlenbergia leptoura is a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the tribe Stipeae and the genus Muhlenbergia. The classification of Muhlenbergia leptoura is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, and the phylum Magnoliophyta. This species is native to Mexico and is part of the flowering plant family. Its classification also includes the class Liliopsida and the order Poales.

intro: Muhlenbergia leptoura, also known as Desert Hair Grass or Muhly Grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Poaceae family. This plant is valued for its traditional medicinal uses, which include the treatment of various health issues such as fever, inflammation, and digestive problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Muhlenbergia leptoura include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Muhlenbergia leptoura is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as colds, flu, and gastrointestinal disorders.
